pages.raw_text:
it is generally the custom of the cor
poration counsel to draw up city
ordinances, but that the one to be
considered tonight is drawn by railroad
attorneys, not to regulate the scalping
business, but to stop it.


Then he declares the council should
require the written declaration of the
railroad officials that they will supply
the rates talked of, pointing out that
in the past the railroads have swindled
the people of Spokane out of millions
of dollars on right of-way propositions
without giving single instance OF
reduced rates except after the famous
boycott.


Since then, AYERS Mr.. Winston. the
railroads have evaded the laws, failed
to pay taxes, purchased legislators and
done things awful, to the point where
it is evident that respectable and &C.
sponsible business men were buncoed
by shrewd agants, high salaried at
torneys and higher salaried lobbyists.


The paper ends with a threat to car
ry the matter to the supreme court
on constitutional grounds and shows
that the brokers save to Spokane
people at least 500 a day, and that
the city council is asked to take $780,
600 year out of the pockets of the
city's travellers to give to the rail
roads, besides confiscating the property
of honest, lawabiding taxpayers, who
are in business and live here.


All of which will be read to the
city councilmen tonight in opposition
to the anti-scalping bill.
